summary: Indian passport holders need a visa to visit Canada. You can apply online through IRCC for a Temporary Resident Visa (TRV), which allows stays of up to 6 months. Processing typically takes 4-8 weeks when applied online. A valid passport, proof of funds, travel itinerary, and ties to your home country are required.
requirements: Valid Indian passport (minimum 6 months beyond planned stay)
Proof of financial support (bank statements for last 4 months, minimum CAD $10,000 recommended)
Travel itinerary and round-trip flight reservation
Proof of ties to India (employment letter, property documents)
Biometrics (fingerprints and photo at a VAC)
steps: Create an account on the IRCC online portal (canada.ca)
Upload all required documents as PDFs
Pay the visa fee (CAD $100) and biometrics fee (CAD $85) online
Book a biometrics appointment at the nearest VFS Global center in India
Attend biometrics appointment with passport and appointment letter
Wait for processing (track status online via your IRCC account)
Once approved, your passport will be stamped or you'll receive a COPR
Travel to Canada within the validity period
tips: Apply at least 3 months before your planned travel date
Show strong ties to India — employment, property, family
Bank balance alone isn't enough; show consistent income over 6 months
If you've traveled to the US, UK, or Schengen countries before, mention it — it helps
Consider applying for a 10-year multiple-entry visa if you plan to visit often

embassyInfo.name: High Commission of Canada in New Delhi

faqs.question: Can I work in Canada with a visitor visa?
faqs.answer: No, a Temporary Resident Visa (visitor visa) does not permit you to work in Canada. You would need a separate work permit for employment.

faqs.question: How long can I stay in Canada on a visitor visa?
faqs.answer: Typically up to 6 months. The exact duration is determined by the border officer upon entry. You can apply for an extension if needed before your authorized stay expires.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa if I'm just transiting through Canada?
faqs.answer: If you hold an Indian passport, yes — you need either a transit visa or an eTA. If you have a valid US visa or green card and are transiting through a Canadian airport, you may qualify for the Transit Without Visa program.

faqs.question: What is the visa refusal rate for Indian applicants?
faqs.answer: Canada's overall visitor visa refusal rate fluctuates but has been around 30-40% for Indian applicants. Strong documentation of ties to India and financial stability significantly improves approval chances.

faqs.question: Can I apply for a Canada visa from a country other than India?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can apply from any country where you are legally residing. However, processing times and requirements may vary. Applying from your home country is generally recommended.

seo.title: India to Canada Visa Requirements 2026 | Indian Passport Holders Guide
seo.description: Complete guide for Indian passport holders traveling to Canada. Visa requirements, application process, documents needed, processing time, fees, and expert tips for 2026.
